title: The Governor has signed Assemblymember Alex Lee’s bill to improve access to safe drinking water in disadvantaged communities. AB 2454 will protect the health and wellbeing of tenan
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2454 will protect the health and wellbeing of tenants who rely on well water.",
    "Domestic well owners must participate in free well testing programs if their well serves a rental property.",
    "Domestic well owners are required to provide the test results to their tenants.",
    "Residents must be provided with potable water if their well water fails to meet safe drinking water standards.",
    "California currently lacks explicit protections for tenants who rely on domestic wells for drinking water.",
    "Neither state nor federal law requires domestic wells to be tested for drinking water contaminants.",
    "It is estimated that over 1.3 million people statewide use domestic wells.",
    "Disadvantaged communities commonly face significant water-quality problems.",
    "Smaller, older, and poorly maintained water systems disproportionately contain contaminants associated with a broad range of health issues, including cancer and gastrointestinal illness.",
    "The counties with the greatest number of domestic wells in high water quality risk areas include Fresno, Sonoma, San Joaquin, and Tulare counties.",
    "When contaminants are found, both interim solutions and remediation strategies can be expensive.",
    "Tenants in rural areas often pay out of pocket for contaminated water and replacement drinking water.",
    "Several local and state funded programs exist to offer services such as well testing and bottled water delivery free of charge.",
    "Well owners with rental units have been reported to decline participation in well testing programs.",
    "AB 2454 will increase participation in ongoing well testing programs.",
    "Domestic well testing programs are often the only way to know if there’s a problem with water quality."
  ]
}
```
